children.heading: .—Applications To Use Vessels
children.label: a .—Applications To Use Vessels
children.text: The Governor of a State sponsoring a State maritime academy (or the Governor of the State designated to conduct the affairs of a regional maritime academy) may apply in writing to the Secretary of Transportation to obtain the use of a training vessel for the academy. A vessel provided under this section remains the property of the United States Government.

children.heading: .—General Authority
children.label: b .—General Authority
children.text: Subject to subsection (c), the Secretary may provide to a State maritime academy, for use as a training vessel, a suitable vessel under the control of the Secretary or made available to the Secretary under subsection (e). If a suitable vessel is not available, the Secretary may build and provide a suitable vessel.
an application has been made under subsection (a);
the State maritime academy satisfies ; andsection 51506(a) of this title
a suitable port will be available for the safe mooring of the vessel while the academy is using the vessel.
repaired, reconditioned, and equipped (with all apparel, charts, books, and instruments of navigation) as necessary for use as a training vessel; and
maintained in good repair by the Secretary.
is suitable for training purposes; and
can be provided without detriment to the service to which the vessel is assigned.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Subject to the availability of appropriations, the Secretary shall pay to each State maritime academy the costs of fuel used by a vessel provided under this section while used for training.

children.label: A
children.text: $100,000 for fiscal year 2006;

children.label: B
children.text: $200,000 for fiscal year 2007; and

children.label: C
children.text: $300,000 for fiscal year 2008 and each fiscal year thereafter.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the date of enactment of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2019, the Secretary, acting through the Maritime Administrator, shall upon consultation with the maritime academies, and to the extent feasible with the consent of the maritime academies, implement a program of training vessel sharing, requiring maritime academies to share training vessel provided by the Secretary among maritime academies, as necessary to ensure that training needs of each academy are met.1So in original. Probably should be “vessels”.1

children.label: A
children.text: ways to maximize the available underway training available in the fleet of training vessels;

children.label: B
children.text: coordinating the dates and duration of training cruises with the academic calendars of maritime academies;

children.label: C
children.text: coordinating academic programs designed to be implemented aboard training vessels among maritime academies; and

children.label: D
children.text: identifying ways to minimize costs.

children.heading: .—Additional funding
children.label: 3 .—Additional funding
children.text: Subject to the availability of appropriations, the Maritime Administrator may provide additional funding to State maritime academies during periods of limited training vessel capacity, for costs associated with training vessel sharing.

children.heading: .—Evaluation
children.label: 4 .—Evaluation
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the beginning of each fiscal year, the Secretary, acting through the Maritime Administrator, shall evaluate the vessel sharing program under this subsection to determine the optimal utilization of State maritime training vessels, and modify the program as necessary to improve utilization.
